# Essentials project profile
<!-- Generated by /first-run. Read first by the essentials agents/skills. Edit freely — it's yours. -->

## Stack
- Language: TypeScript ~6.0.3 — strict mode enabled (noImplicitAny, noUnusedLocals, noUnusedParameters)
- Framework: React 19.2.3 + React Native 0.85.3 + Expo SDK ~56.0.12
- Router: Expo Router ~56.2.11 (file-based routing, typed routes enabled)
- Styling: NativeWind 4.2.6 + Tailwind CSS 3.4.19
- State: Zustand 5.0.14 (local/client) + @tanstack/react-query 5.101.1 (server/fetched)
- Backend: Supabase JS 2.108.2 (Apple Sign-In auth; Supabase DB integration planned but not yet active)
- Package manager: npm (legacy-peer-deps=true in .npmrc; package-lock.json present)
- Data layer: Local JSON file (data/restaurants.json) via LocalRestaurantRepository; Supabase backend planned (swap is zero-UI-change via IRestaurantRepository interface)

## Commands
- Lint: `npm run lint` → `eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx --ignore-path .gitignore`
- Format: `npm run format` → `prettier --write "**/*.{ts,tsx,js,json}"`
- Typecheck: `npx tsc --noEmit` (no explicit script — inferred from tsconfig strict config)
- Build (iOS): `npx expo run:ios --device "00008150-000A0DCA22E8C01C"` (Zach's iPhone)
- Run (dev): `npm start` → `expo start`
- Test: (none configured — no jest/vitest, no test scripts in package.json)

## Layout
- Source: `app/` (screens, Expo Router file-based), `components/` (ui/ primitives + restaurant/ + wasabi/), `stores/` (Zustand), `services/` (business logic), `repositories/` (data access), `hooks/`, `types/`, `constants/`, `utils/`, `lib/`
- Data: `data/restaurants.json` — JSON database of 26 AYCE restaurants (source of truth for all restaurant data)
- Tests: none (no test files, no runner, no __tests__ dirs in source)
- Skip: `node_modules/`, `.expo/`, `ios/`, `android/`, `dist/`, `web-build/`, `.git/`, `assets/`

## Conventions
- **No `any` types** — `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any: error` (hard lint error, not just warning)
- **No inline styles** — `react-native/no-inline-styles: warn` (prefer StyleSheet or NativeWind className)
- **Use `@` path aliases** — never relative imports; e.g. `@components/ui/Text`, `@stores/authStore`, `@utils/hours`
- **Typography via `<Text variant="…">`** — never import raw React Native `<Text>` in screens/components; use the design-system wrapper in `components/ui/Text.tsx` (7 variants: heading1/2/3, body, bodySmall, caption, label)
- **Colors from `useColors()`** — never hardcode theme colors; use the hook which returns light/dark-aware values
- **Repository pattern** — data access only through `IRestaurantRepository`; `RestaurantService` is the only caller; UI never imports from `repositories/` directly
- **Zustand for client state, React Query for fetched data** — don't mix them; useRestaurants() / useRestaurant() are the canonical hooks for restaurant data
- **Stores use SecureStore persistence** — `authStore` stores user identity in expo-secure-store; don't store sensitive data in AsyncStorage
- **Prettier config**: single quotes, trailing commas everywhere, 100-char line width, 2-space indent, arrow parens always
- **CRITICAL — Expo v56**: Always read https://docs.expo.dev/versions/v56.0.0/ before writing any Expo/React Native API code; APIs changed significantly in SDK 56 / New Architecture
- **New Architecture (JSI) enabled** — avoid bridge-based patterns; use the JSI-compatible APIs
- **Haptic feedback** on interactive actions via `expo-haptics` (already used consistently throughout the app)
- **Animated entrance patterns** — new list items use staggered `withDelay(index * 60ms)` translateY + opacity animations via react-native-reanimated

## Domain
SushiMap is a mobile iOS app for discovering All-You-Can-Eat (AYCE) sushi restaurants in the NYC metro area and North Jersey. Core entities: `Restaurant` (address, coordinates, lunch/dinner prices, time limit, hours, tags, photos, BYOB flag, sashimi/nigiri availability), `JournalEntry` (user visit logs with multi-dimensional ratings: fish quality, value, service, refill speed, atmosphere), passport stamps (gamified visit tracking with collections and achievements), and saved restaurants. Authentication is Apple Sign-In only (guest mode supported). Data is currently served from a local JSON file; Supabase is wired for auth and planned for the data layer.
